high-Tech breakthrougks: ThE BATTLEMECH from the Abridged Encyclopedia Galactica  Universal Press; 3062 With Its debut in 2439, the BattleMech radically altered the way humans waged war: BattlaMechs were faster; more maneu- verable and more versatile than conventional tanks; and com- bat commanders Immediately recognized their value: Even nOW, six centuries later; the 'Mech remains "king of the battlatield: Generally humanoid In appearance (though several mod: els use less conventional four-legged design) , BattleMechs commonly range from ten to fourteen meters in height and weigh anywhere from 20 t0 100 tons. An intricate skeleton of titanium   alloy 'bones"  forms the   'Mech s   basic   structure. Around It are attached 'muscles made of bundled fibers, called myomers. These myomers expand or contract like human mus cles when exposed to varying levels of electrical current, giving the 'Mech its Impressive  strength and mobility: "skin' hardened armor; varying thickness , makes the "Mech virtual- Iy invulnerable to small arms and provides considerable pro- tection against heavier weapons_ Fusion reactors   fumish the energy needed to power the Mech s systems and myomers_ Each BattleMech also mounts massive gyroscope, giving the stability t0 move its mass. device known as neurohelmet picks Up the neural impulses of the pilot and transmits them to the 'Mech's control systems. The pilot uses his Or her own sense of balance keep the BattleMech upright and can make constant corrections to the vehicle $ move_ ments with degree of control impossible for joysticks and foot pedals alone . Heat sinks, interconnected by complex cooling system , dissipate the massive amounts of heat the 'Mech gener- ates. BattleMechs are designed for combat and can mount sev- weapons andor electronics packages. Some are built with jump jets that allow them to jump over obstacles new development, introduced to the Inner Sphere by the Clans, permits technicians to quickly modify the configuration of specially designed BattleMechs series of common con- nections and mounting hardpoints allows these OmniMechs to carry wide variety of weapons and otnet equipment, which can be easily attached and disconnected . OmniMech technolo- gy allows battlefield commanders t0 fine-tune their forces on the fly to meet unexpected challenges.
LOOKING TOWARD DAWN From Putting Down the Bully , Andrea Paliwoda; ComStar Archives, 3062 The reborn Star League defeated the most powerful Crusader Clan on its own homeworld because we finally bridged the technology gap: Clan weapons are still more powerful and more efficient than ours_ and the Clans have far better produc- tion capabilities_ But more and more of our factories are retool ing their assembly lines every day to build advanced weapons and the vehicles to carry them into battle. The Clans retain their technological superiority; and Mikely will for some time_ The Inner Sphere has come long way in the past four decades- possibly the quickest renaissance in the history humankind but are still decades benind Kerensky' descendants, and the Periphery realms are in even worse shape. The most advanced of them has barely begun to produce equipment the Successor States have had decades while the technological level of the average Periphery nation is below that of the Inner Sphere at the height the Succession wars.
DAILY LIFE Throughout human-occupied space the basics of daily life remain the same On every world people get up in the morning; go t0 school work take personal or family time and usually spend less than five minutes contemplating their place in the larger scheme of things_ The details, however; can make all the difterence the   technologically advanced core  worlds of  each Inner Sphere nation_ technology is taken for granted even if especially if_it vulnerable JumpShips HPG communica tions holovision_ barely understood computers all of these are easily ost and impossible to replace  nard truth hidden beneath carefully crafted facade of superiority: For citizens of these worlds politics and military concerns arc more topics tor civil debate than for Iife-Or-death decisions. Deprivation most often makes itself felt as mere inconvenience t0 daily routine Or shortage of luxury items_ Most of the other Inner Sphere worlds_ as well as the most advanced Periphery worlds, more clearly understand the cariousness of the Inner Sphere'= technology: On such worlds, everyday circumstances make people more aware of the frailty of technology: Businessmen may converse via cell phone on their way t0 work but they get there riding norse Or donkey: The latest luxury hovercratt may be parked next to vehicle fueled by methane collected from its owner's farm. Many these 'middle class worlds rely on foodstuffs r other critical shipments from nearby planets The specter of war looms heav ily; raids are part of life_ and some areas still bear the scars of battle. Finally; there the Outback: the collective name for worlds that suffer constant deprivation due their remote location
possible for humans t0 Iive on even the most hostile planets: The Clans created eugenics program that birthed thousands of genetically enhanced babies every year ad devised weapons of war capable of devastating damage. Naturally; tney cut swath hundreds of light-years across through the Inner Sphere We fought them the only way we could: dirty. But we knew that if we hoped to defeat thc Clans had to level the technological playing field. So we assigned scientists to rip apart every bit of Clan technology we cap
